vulnerability.title: Configure Accepting Router Advertisements on All IPv6 Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra = 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi done # # Set sysctl co

triage_decision.reason: This is a sysctl parameter change for IPv6 security hardening. According to policy, sysctl persistence changes are safe to remediate as they follow standard STIG hardening patterns and are unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage in typical server environments.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; filesystems affected; Potential IPv6 connectivity issues if router advertisements are required for network operation

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for sysctl_net_ipv6_conf_all_accept_ra (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sysctl_net_ipv6_conf_all_accept_ra). Could not verify.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
The finding is about configuring the sysctl parameter `net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra` to 0. This is a security hardening measure to disable accepting IPv6 router advertisements.

According to the remediation guidance:
1. Set the runtime value: `sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0`
2. Make it persistent by adding `net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra = 0` to a file in `/etc/sysctl.d/`

I need to:
1. First check the current value with `sysctl net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra`
2. Then set it to 0 with `sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0`
3. Check existing sysctl configuration files to see if there's already a setting for this
4. Add or modify the setting in a persistent file (likely `/etc/sysctl.d/99-rocky.conf` or similar)

Let me start by reading the current sysctl value and inspecting the sysctl configuration files.
